name: batch-read
description: Read multiple files or line ranges in one invocation, optionally with line numbers, count, pattern filtering via --grep, context around matches via -C, --code-only to strip source file boilerplate, or --structure to list functions/classes/structs.

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: One or more file paths, optionally with :start-end line ranges

parameters.name: --head=N
parameters.description: Show first N lines of each file

parameters.name: --tail=N
parameters.description: Show last N lines of each file

parameters.name: --lines=start-end
parameters.description: Show line range start-end for subsequent files

parameters.name: --count
parameters.description: Show line count of each file (like wc -l)

parameters.name: --grep=PATTERN
parameters.description: Show only lines matching an extended regex pattern

parameters.name: -C=N, --context=N
parameters.description: Show N lines of context around --grep matches

parameters.name: --code-only
parameters.description: Strip package/import boilerplate from Go/Python source files

parameters.name: --dir=PATH
parameters.description: Read all files in a directory, use with --include

parameters.name: --cd=DIR
parameters.description: Change to DIR before reading files (replaces cd + read pattern)

examples.expected: First 50 lines of file1.py, then lines 100-200 of file2.py

cost_saving_rationale: Collapses multiple cat/nl/sed-n/wc/grep/code-structure calls into one step, saving step costs and observation size; --cd replaces cd + read two-step pattern.
